items.final_wording: Supports documenting chronic pain, opioid use, medicine history, psychosocial context, alcohol or illicit substance context, function, and specialist involvement.

source.title: Surgery and Opioids: Best Practice Guidelines 2021
source.population: People undergoing surgery, including people with chronic pain or preoperative opioid use.
source.setting: Preoperative assessment, perioperative planning, PACU, discharge, pain service, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting chronic pain, current opioid use, medicine and psychosocial or substance history, functional pain assessment, clinician-entered perioperative plan, complex pain review, discharge communication, and pain-service follow-up. Medication changes, doses, and tapering are not mapped; scheduled review is overdue.

exact_location.heading: Preoperative assessment of pain and opioid use

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting chronic pain, opioid use, medicine history, psychosocial context, alcohol or illicit substance context, function, and specialist involvement.

items.rationale: The exact CPOC section supports recording current opioid use, chronic pain, medicine and psychosocial history, alcohol or illicit substance context, and specialist involvement before surgery.

items.final_wording: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

items.rationale: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Perioperative opioid use documentation; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
